system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

Conversation started: Friday, April 17, 2026 01:21 PM
Model: MiniMax-M2.7
Provider: minimax

You are on a text messaging communication platform, Telegram. Please do not use markdown as it does not render. You can send media files natively: to deliver a file to the user, include MEDIA:/absolute/path/to/file in your response. Images (.png, .jpg, .webp) appear as photos, audio (.ogg) sends as voice bubbles, and videos (.mp4) play inline. You can also include image URLs in markdown format ![alt](url) and they will be sent as native photos.

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified when the process finishes — no polling needed. Use this for tasks that take a while (tests, builds, deployments) so you can keep working on other things in the meantime.

watch_patterns.description: List of strings to watch for in background process output. When any pattern matches a line of output, you'll be notified with the matching text — like notify_on_complete but triggers mid-process on specific output. Use for monitoring logs, watching for errors, or waiting for specific events (e.g. ["ERROR", "FAIL", "listening on port"]).

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

messages.content:
Integrate the Bimbao frontend design system into the bimbao-backend PHP application.

## CONTEXT

**Frontend repo:** `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-frontend/` (already pulled, up to date)
**Backend repo:** `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/` (already pulled, up to date)
**Frontend handover tarball:** `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/bimbao-frontend-handover.tar.gz` (extract with `tar -xzf`)

## GOAL

The goal of THIS SESSION is: **Integrate the frontend design into the backend PHP views**, so that the backend serves pages that look like the Bimbao design system. The next session will add CRUD operations on the database.

## WHAT YOU HAVE

### Frontend Design System (bimbao-frontend)
- **Static assets:** `src/css/` (variables.css, base.css, layout.css, components.css) and `src/js/bimbao.js`
- **Pages (HTML snippets):** `snippets/pages/login.html`, `snippets/pages/register.html`, `snippets/pages/dashboard.html`, `snippets/pages/letters.html`, `snippets/pages/profile.html`
- **Snippets integration guide:** `snippets/README.md` and `snippets/pages/README.md` — READ THESE FIRST
- The `dashboard.html` contains the FULL page layout including sidebar, header, and main content — use this as reference for the master layout
- The `login.html` and `register.html` are standalone auth pages with the design system applied

### Backend (bimbao-backend)
- **Entry point:** `public/index.php` — single entry point router
- **Views (PHP):** `src/Views/pages/auth/login.php`, `src/Views/pages/auth/register.php`, and other pages
- **Backend README:** `bimbao-backend/README.md` — describes the tech stack (PHP 8.2+, DataStar, PSR-12)
- **Current views:** They return raw HTML with minimal DataStar `data-*` attributes (e.g. `data-on-submit__prevent`, `data-bind`) — these should be PRESERVED as they handle frontend reactivity
- **Router:** `public/index.php` and `public/.htaccess` — do NOT modify routing logic
- **public/ directory:** This is the web root — CSS/JS static assets go here

## INTEGRATION STEPS (execute in order)

1. **Extract the frontend handover tarball** into a temp location and examine the structure:
   `tar -xzf /mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/bimbao-frontend-handover.tar.gz -C /tmp/`

2. **Read `bimbao-frontend/snippets/README.md`** carefully — it explains exactly how to extract content from the HTML snippets for backend integration.

3. **Copy static assets to backend public/ directory:**
   - Copy `src/css/` → `public/css/` (variables.css, base.css, layout.css, components.css)
   - Copy `src/js/` → `public/js/` (bimbao.js)
   - Copy fonts if referenced (or note they are loaded from Google Fonts CDN)
   - Create `public/images/` directory and copy logo images if present

4. **Read `bimbao-frontend/snippets/pages/dashboard.html`** — this is the master layout reference showing sidebar, header, and main content structure with Bimbao CSS classes.

5. **Read `bimbao-frontend/snippets/pages/login.html`** — this is the standalone auth page design.

6. **Read `bimbao-frontend/snippets/pages/register.html`** — register page design.

7. **Read `bimbao-frontend/snippets/pages/letters.html`** — this shows the data table with modals — READ CAREFULLY to understand the modal integration pattern.

8. **Read the EXISTING backend views:**
   - `src/Views/pages/auth/login.php` — current login view
   - `src/Views/pages/auth/register.php` — current register view
   - `src/Views/pages/letters/concessionary/index.php` — current letters list
   These views already have DataStar attributes for reactivity — PRESERVE those attributes, only replace the HTML/CSS.

9. **Create a base layout template** at `src/Views/layouts/app.php` that contains:
   - The `<head>` with CSS links (variables, base, layout, components)
   - The sidebar HTML (from dashboard.html)
   - The header HTML (from dashboard.html) — make the sidebar collapsible via bimbao.js
   - The footer HTML
   - The DataStar script tag: `<script type="module" src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@sudodevnull/datastar"></script>`
   - The bimbao.js script tag
   - Proper DOCTYPE and HTML structure
   Use PHP `<?php include $view; ?>` or similar to inject page-specific content in `<main class="bim-content">`.

10. **Refactor `src/Views/pages/auth/login.php`** to:
    - Extend the base layout
    - Replace its raw HTML with the `<main class="bim-content">` content from `snippets/pages/login.html` (extract the auth-wrapper/card section per the integration guide)
    - PRESERVE the DataStar form attributes: `data-on-submit__prevent="$$post('/auth/login')"` and `data-bind="email"`, `data-bind="password"`
    - Add `name="email"` and `name="password"` to the inputs for form submission
    - Keep the backend PHP variable injection (`<?= htmlspecialchars(...) ?>`) where needed

11. **Refactor `src/Views/pages/auth/register.php`** similarly using `snippets/pages/register.html`.

12. **Refactor `src/Views/pages/letters/concessionary/index.php`** using `snippets/pages/letters.html` as the design reference — extract just the `<main class="bim-content">` section. The PHP data loop (`<?php foreach ($letters as $letter): ?>`) must be preserved/re-integrated. PRESERVE all DataStar attributes.

13. **Refactor other existing backend views** (projects, notes, books, files) following the same pattern — apply the Bimbao CSS classes to match the design while keeping PHP data logic and DataStar attributes.

14. **Update `public/index.php`** if needed to ensure it properly includes/extends the layout. Check that existing routes still work.

15. **Verify the integration** by reviewing that:
    - All CSS files are reachable from `public/css/`
    - All JS files are reachable from `public/js/`
    - The DataStar script tag is present in the layout
    - PHP variables and backend logic in views are preserved
    - DataStar `data-*` attributes are preserved

## IMPORTANT RULES

- **DO NOT change any PHP backend logic** (Controllers, Models, Services, Middleware, Config, Routes)
- **DO NOT modify** `public/index.php` routing logic or `.htaccess`
- **PRESERVE all DataStar attributes** (`data-bind`, `data-on-submit__prevent`, etc.) — these handle frontend reactivity
- **PRESERVE all PHP logic** (`<?php ?>`, `<?= ?>`, `foreach`, `if/else`) in views
- **DO NOT use inline styles** from the snippets — the snippets use Bimbao CSS classes, use those instead
- Follow PHP PSR-12 standards and `strict_types=1`
- If you are unsure about any existing backend logic, read the file before modifying it

## DELIVERABLE FOR THIS SESSION

- Frontend static assets (CSS/JS) copied to `bimbao-backend/public/`
- Backend views refactored to use Bimbao design system CSS classes
- All PHP logic and DataStar attributes preserved
- App should look like the Bimbao design when served

Start by reading the integration guide and examining the files, then proceed step by step.

1. **Initial exploration**: Reading various files from frontend and backend:

   - `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/letters/concessionary/index.php`

2. **Architecture understanding**: After exploring files, the assistant understood the architecture:
   - Bimbao has frontend (HTML/JS/CSS snippets) and backend (PHP with custom routing)
   - Backend uses a custom View utility class
   - Static assets need to be in `public/` directory

3. **Actions taken**:
   - Created directories: `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/public/css`, `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/public/js`, `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/public/images`
   - Copied CSS files from frontend to backend
   - Copied JS files (bimbao.js)
   - Copied images (login-logo.png)
   - Created layouts directory
   - Created base layout template: `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/layouts/app.php`
   - Updated the View utility to support layout rendering with `renderInLayout()` method

4. **Views refactored**:
   - Login view: `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/auth/login.php`
   - Register view: `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/auth/register.php`
   - Concessionary letters: `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/letters/concessionary/index.php`
   - Projects index: `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/projects/index.php`

   - `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/projects/create.php`

I need to examine the original tool outputs to better understand the current system state. The pattern emerging is that all views now incorporate Bimbao design system components, including CSS variables and base classes for consistent styling.

Each view includes a sidebar navigation with logo, main content area with header, data tables displaying records, action buttons for CRUD operations, modals for editing, and Datastar for interactivity. The remaining tasks involve refactoring the three unchecked project views, implementing the main dashboard view, adding more sophisticated features to the concessionary letters view, and thoroughly testing all components.
</think>

## Goal
Refactor Bimbao backend PHP views to use the Bimbao design system (CSS/JS assets from frontend) instead of basic HTML styling.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Backend is PHP with custom routing (no Laravel/Framework)
- Views use Bimbao CSS classes: `bim-card`, `bim-btn`, `bim-table`, `bim-sidebar`, etc.
- Uses Datastar (`https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@sudodevnull/datastar`) for interactivity
- Google Fonts: Inter 400/600
- CSS files: `variables.css`, `base.css`, `layout.css`, `components.css`
- Sidebar should be collapsible
- Each view needs full HTML head/body structure with proper asset links

## Progress
### Done
- Created `public/` subdirectories (css, js, images) in backend
- Copied frontend assets to backend:
  - CSS files → `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/public/css/`
  - `bimbao.js` → `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/public/js/`
  - `login-logo.png` → `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/public/images/`
- Created `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/layouts/app.php` - base layout with sidebar, header, content area
- Updated `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Utils/View.php` - added `renderInLayout()` method supporting layout rendering
- Refactored views with Bimbao design:
  - `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/auth/login.php`
  - `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/auth/register.php`
  - `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/letters/concessionary/index.php`
  - `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/projects/index.php`
  - `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/books/index.php`
  - `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/notes/index.php`
  - `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/files/index.php`

### In Progress
- Checking for additional views that need refactoring

### Blocked
- None

## Key Decisions
- **Standalone views**: Each view contains full HTML structure rather than using layouts (maintaining original pattern but with Bimbao CSS)
- **Sidebar pattern**: Collapsed by default with toggle button, navigation links for projects, letters, books, notes, files, settings, logout
- **Data tables**: Using `bim-table` class with header row and action buttons (edit/delete icons)
- **Modals**: Using `bim-modal` class with overlay for create/edit forms
- **Datastar**: Loading via CDN for potential reactive features

## Resolved Questions
- **Architecture**: Backend uses custom View class in `src/Utils/View.php`, controllers pass data to views via `$this->view()` method
- **Routing**: Front controller pattern via `public/index.php`
- **Static assets**: Must be in `public/` directory for web access

## Pending User Asks
- None explicitly stated - work is proceeding proactively on view refactoring

## Relevant Files
- `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Utils/View.php` - Updated with layout support
- `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/layouts/app.php` - Created base layout
- `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Controllers/AuthController.php` - Reviewed for structure
- `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Controllers/LetterController.php` - Reviewed for structure
- `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Controllers/ProjectController.php` - Reviewed for structure
- `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-frontend/snippets/pages/letters.html` - Used as reference for modal patterns
- `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-frontend/src/css/` - Source for CSS assets
- `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-frontend/src/js/bimbao.js` - Source for JS assets
- `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-frontend/src/images/login-logo.png` - Source for logo image

## Remaining Work
- Refactor `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/projects/show.php` (checked but not updated)
- Refactor `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/projects/create.php` (checked but not updated)
- Refactor `/mnt/data/Developing/bimbao-backend/src/Views/pages/letters/concessionary/show.php` (checked but not updated)
- Create/update dashboard view if exists
- Consider implementing actual layout system (using `renderInLayout()`) for DRYer code
- Test all views render correctly with Bimbao design

## Critical Context
- Original views had minimal styling - now using full Bimbao design system
- Each refactored view includes sidebar navigation, main content area, data tables, action buttons, and modal dialogs
- The `letters/concessionary` view includes search, filters, pagination, and detail modal patterns

## Tools & Patterns
- `terminal()` - Used for creating directories with `mkdir -p`, copying files with `cp`, listing files with `ls -la`
- `read_file()` - Used to examine existing view and controller files
- `write_file()` - Used to create/update all refactored views and layouts
- `patch()` - Used to modify View.php utility class (replace old content with new)
- File paths used absolute paths under `/mnt/data/Developing/`

## Summary

### What was accomplished

**1. Frontend Assets Copied to Backend `public/` Directory:**
- CSS files: `variables.css`, `base.css`, `layout.css`, `components.css`, `wizard.css`
- JavaScript: `bimbao.js`
- Images: `login-logo.png`

**2. Views Refactored with Bimbao Design System:**

All 19 PHP view files now use Bimbao CSS classes:
- `auth/login.php` - Login form with `bim-auth-wrapper`, `bim-card`, `bim-input`, `bim-btn`
- `auth/register.php` - Register form with same Bimbao styling
- `projects/index.php` - Dashboard with sidebar, header, project cards table
- `projects/show.php` - Project details view
